steps.title: Identify the claim and what evidence must show
steps.content: The claim is that the author emphasizes the setting where Valia is reading is nearly silent. The correct quotation must directly depict that silence — not cold, not book size, not Valia’s actions.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘"The hand in which he carried his book was getting stiff with cold, but he would not ask his mother to take the book from him."’: Describes physical discomfort from cold, not silence. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"Valia was reading a huge, very huge book, almost half as large as himself."’: Emphasizes the size of the book, not any quality of the setting. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"Valia approached the window and examined the toys."’: Describes Valia’s action at a window, with no reference to sound or silence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘"Everything in the room was quiet, so quiet that the only thing to be heard was the rustling of the pages he turned."’: Uses “quiet” twice and names the only audible sound, directly illustrating near silence. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
